name: Relaxion
summary: A slowly-rolling scalar field (the relaxion) scans the Higgs mass through cosmological evolution. As the relaxion rolls, the effective Higgs mass-squared decreases past zero; once electroweak symmetry breaks, the Higgs VEV backreacts on the relaxion potential and stops the roll. The mechanism explains the smallness of m_H without new physics at the EW scale.

addresses_puzzles.note: Replaces fine-tuning with a long inflationary epoch and a specific relaxion potential. Requires very large e-foldings (>~10^36) and a sub-Planckian relaxion field range; viability depends on the underlying axion-like UV completion.

touches_benchmarks.note: Generic relaxion does not modify Higgs couplings significantly; specific implementations with relaxion-Higgs mixing predict O(0.01) deviations.

new_fields: auxiliary new strong sector to generate backreaction
introduces.note: Scalar potential slope, backreaction scale, initial conditions, and inflationary scale during the scan.

history: Graham, Kaplan, Rajendran (2015). Variants with QCD or non-QCD backreaction proliferated through 2016-2018; cosmological consistency (overproduction of inflationary perturbations, baryogenesis compatibility) is the leading concern.

references.citation: Graham, Kaplan, Rajendran, 'Cosmological relaxation of the electroweak scale', PRL 115, 221801 (2015)
